forecast-period.text: A low pressure trough over central districts will move southeastwards and slip south of SA early today. A series of low pressure systems passing through the Bight during the week will extend cold fronts that will cross over SA through to the end of the week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Tuesday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly in the early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 30 knots west of Kangaroo Island in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Westerly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm at night.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Wednesday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning northwesterly 25 to 30 knots during the morning. Winds reaching up to 35 knots west of Kangaroo Island in the late evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, tending westerly 4 to 7 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
